preamble.summary: Enoch sees frankincense trees and other aromatic plants in the eastern mountains.
preamble.remarkable: The catalogue of aromatic plants connects to the incense traditions of the temple and the luxury trade routes of the ancient Near East.
preamble.connections: Exodus 30:34 (frankincense in sacred incense); Matthew 2:11 (frankincense as gift); Revelation 18:13 (frankincense in trade).

verses.text_reference: Then I went to another place in the desert, and approached to the east of this mountain range.
verses.rendering: Then I went to another place in the wilderness and approached the eastern side of this mountain range.

verses.text_reference: And there I saw aromatic trees exhaling the fragrance of frankincense and myrrh, and the trees also were similar to the almond tree.
verses.rendering: There I saw aromatic trees giving off the fragrance of frankincense and myrrh, and the trees resembled almond trees.
translator_notes: Frankincense and myrrh together evoke both the temple incense (Exodus 30:34) and the gifts brought to Jesus (Matthew 2:11). The almond tree (Hebrew: shaqed) is associated with God's watchfulness in Jeremiah 1:11-12.
